If a different display format is preferred, adjust the alias expression accordingly.","Downstream SQL should perform GROUP BY professors.id, professors.first_name, professors.last_name and may include HAVING num_evaluations >= N if a minimum-evaluations threshold is desired (not applied here).","Rating scales (e.g., 1-5) are not normalized or re-scaled; averages are raw arithmetic means of integer rating columns."]},"attempt":2,"maxAttempts":4,"previousAttempt":{"sql":"WITH per_prof AS (
  SELECT
    p.id AS professor_id,
    p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name AS professor_name,
    AVG(ce.content_rating)::numeric(10,2) AS avg_content_rating,
    AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric(10,2) AS avg_prof_rating,
    COUNT(ce.id) AS num_evaluations
  FROM public.course_evaluations ce
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
  WHERE ce.content_rating IS NOT NULL
    AND ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
  ORDER BY p.last_name, p.first_name
)

visualization.title: Average content rating vs average professor rating (per professor)

resultRaw.sql:
WITH per_prof AS (
  SELECT
    p.id AS professor_id,
    p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name AS professor_name,
    AVG(ce.content_rating)::numeric(10,2) AS avg_content_rating,
    AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric(10,2) AS avg_prof_rating,
    COUNT(ce.id) AS num_evaluations
  FROM public.course_evaluations ce
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
  WHERE ce.content_rating IS NOT NULL
    AND ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
  ORDER BY p.last_name, p.first_name
)
SELECT professor_id, professor_name, avg_content_rating, avg_prof_rating, num_evaluations
FROM per_prof;

issues: All encoded fields (avg_content_rating, avg_prof_rating, professor_name, num_evaluations, professor_id) are present in metadata — good.
Coloring by professor_name will create one category per professor (20 in sample), which can reduce readability; consider removing color or using labels/interactive highlighting for many professors.
The visualization adds size encoding (num_evaluations) which was not requested but is a sensible enhancement — note as a non-blocking deviation.

visualization.title: Average content rating vs average professor rating (per professor)

result.sql:
WITH per_prof AS (
  SELECT
    p.id AS professor_id,
    p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name AS professor_name,
    AVG(ce.content_rating)::numeric(10,2) AS avg_content_rating,
    AVG(ce.professor_rating)::numeric(10,2) AS avg_prof_rating,
    COUNT(ce.id) AS num_evaluations
  FROM public.course_evaluations ce
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON ce.enrollment_id = e.id
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.professors p ON cs.professor_id = p.id
  WHERE ce.content_rating IS NOT NULL
    AND ce.professor_rating IS NOT NULL
  GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
  ORDER BY p.last_name, p.first_name
)
SELECT professor_id, professor_name, avg_content_rating, avg_prof_rating, num_evaluations
FROM per_prof;

issues: All encoded fields (avg_content_rating, avg_prof_rating, professor_name, num_evaluations, professor_id) are present in metadata — good.
Coloring by professor_name will create one category per professor (20 in sample), which can reduce readability; consider removing color or using labels/interactive highlighting for many professors.
The visualization adds size encoding (num_evaluations) which was not requested but is a sensible enhancement — note as a non-blocking deviation.
